title: selaginella huehuetenangensis
intro: Selaginella huehuetenangensis, also known as Resurrection Fern, is a type of spore-bearing vascular plant that belongs to the Selaginellaceae family. It is native to Mexico and Central America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules, which are used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
